description:
The literal mutator currently emits mutation sites without validating that
the resulting AST will compile or run. Two known cases:

## Case 1: Capture arguments flipped to &0

`&1`, `&2`, ... get flipped to `&0` by the literal mutator. Captures require
args numbered 1-255; `&0` fails at compile time. Counted as `compile_error`
rather than a meaningful mutant.

Reproductions:
- v1: `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex` (`&MapSet.member?(candidates, &1)`),
  also two instances in `ast.ex`
- v2 (builder, 2026-05-18 study): reproduced again

## Case 2: Range step flipped to 0

A `Range` step literal (`a..b//1` or `Range.new(a, b, 1)`) gets flipped to `0`.
`Range.new(a, b, 0)` raises `ArgumentError: cannot create range with step 0`
at runtime — the mutation makes it past compile but blows up before tests
run, so it's classified as a runtime error rather than a meaningful mutant.

Reproduction:
- Builder study (2026-05-18). Specific file location captured in the per-run
  JSON at `/tmp/builder-mutagen-runs/`.

## Why this matters

These show up as noise in the JSON output (compile_error or aborted-run
buckets), and they inflate "mutagen failure mode" counts in adopter
dashboards. They are not test gaps — they are invalid mutations the mutator
should never have emitted. The kill_rate metric ignores them, so the
correctness impact is small, but the trust signal to adopters is bad
("why is mutagen producing nonsense mutations?").

## Proposed fix

In `MutagenEx.Mutators.Literal`, when enumerating a literal site, check
whether the literal occurs inside a context where 0 is structurally invalid:

1. **Capture context.** If the parent AST node is `:&` (capture) and the
   literal is a positional integer reference, never emit `0` as a target.
2. **Range-step context.** If the literal occurs in a `..//step` AST or
   inside a `Range.new(_, _, step)` call as the third arg, never emit `0`
   as a target. Also consider banning `step` mutations that would invert
   sign without flipping the endpoints (`1..10//-1` is a valid but empty
   range — equivalent mutant territory).

General principle: pre-flight every (site, candidate) pair through a
syntactic validity check before emitting it. Mutators that emit syntactic
junk reduce confidence in the killable/survivable numbers.

## Evidence

- v1 study failure-modes section (Cases 1):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/mutagen_ex/2026-05-17-efficacy-study.md
- Builder study failure-modes section (Cases 1 and 2):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/builder/2026-05-18-efficacy-study.md
- Cross-project synthesis Findings 4.2 and 4.3

## Acceptance

- Test fixture: a module with `&MapSet.member?(set, &1)` and `1..10//2`
  produces zero sites for capture-arg-zero and range-step-zero mutations.
- Other literal mutations on the same expressions still work (e.g. `&1`
  in non-capture context still mutates).

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation (no implementation yet)

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal/**.ex (if helpers carved out)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
- test/fixtures/**/*.ex (new fixture with captures/ranges allowed)
- .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md (only if a requirement must be recorded)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex (other mutators — do not touch)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_*.ex

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not change mutator dispatch / registration logic.
- Do not refactor other mutators while in this file.
- Existing non-capture / non-range literal mutations must keep producing the same sites.
- No new external runtime deps.

Verification command: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

WORKER_ERROR
Session: codex-274c8e7b
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-literal-mutator-validate/.codex-runs/mutagen-z9k.log

Reason: The scope annotation is too narrow for the requested fix. `MutagenEx.Mutators.Literal.match?/1`, `mutate/1`, and `validate/1` receive only the current AST node or swapped AST node; the invalid cases require knowing the parent/context (`{:&, _, [n]}`, `{:..//, _, [_, _, step]}`, and `Range.new(_, _, step)`). Bare integer child nodes are what currently produce the invalid `1 -> 0` and step `1/2 -> 0` sites, and `literal.ex` cannot distinguish those from ordinary non-capture/non-range integer literals without context from `MutationEnumerator` or dispatch traversal.

Scope conflict: Correct implementation needs an allowed change to pass parent/path context into literal validation or to let the enumerator filter context-sensitive invalid literal candidates before emitting sites. The scope annotation explicitly marks `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` and other dispatch/registration logic out of scope, so widening silently would violate the ticket rules.

ORCHESTRATOR: scope annotation revised (replaces prior)

Reason: the prior annotation was too narrow. The pre-validation requires
parent/path context that lives in the mutation enumerator, not in
literal.ex alone. Widening scope on this ticket rather than carving a
subticket because it is one cohesive change.

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal/**.ex (if helpers carved out)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (newly allowed — to thread parent
  AST context into literal validation OR to filter invalid candidates
  before emission)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex (newly allowed — only if mutator dispatch
  signature must change to accept context; keep the change minimal)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/literal_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs (newly allowed — only if
  enumerator behavior changed and needs coverage)
- test/fixtures/**/*.ex (new fixture with captures/ranges allowed)
- .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md (only if a requirement must be recorded)
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md (only if a requirement must
  be recorded)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex other than literal.ex (other mutators —
  do not touch their internals)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_*.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not refactor other mutators (arithmetic, boolean, etc.) — even if
  passing through.
- Existing non-capture / non-range literal mutations must keep producing
  the same sites (regression check).
- Do not change the public API of MutagenEx.Mutators dispatch beyond
  what's strictly necessary to thread context.
- No new external runtime deps.

Verification command: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test

title: Literal mutator: pre-validate AST shape (Range step, capture args)
